page: Amazon Web Services i8ge.12xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor. Running on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance features 48 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, operating at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 384 GB of system memory, providing 8 GB of RAM per core. The hardware configuration includes 30,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps. There are no GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses bundled with this instance.

In benchmark evaluations, the i8ge.12xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core and memory performance. The system achieved top 10% rankings in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, Geekbench multi-core scores, and Passmark database operations. Large-block memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 389.45 GB/sec for read-only and 192.74 GB/sec for write operations. Multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and Redis SET operations are also strong, placing in the top 25%. However, single-core performance is moderate, with Geekbench single-core and stress-ng single-core scores falling in the middle 50% range.

The instance exhibits a clear tradeoff between massive parallel processing and single-threaded execution. While multi-threaded tasks like software compilation and database operations excel, single-threaded operations such as gzip compression and small-model LLM text generation are relatively weaker, with the latter placing in the bottom 25%. Given the combination of 30,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, high memory bandwidth, and 48 dedicated ARM64 cores, this instance is highly cost-efficient for storage-heavy, memory-intensive, and highly parallelized workloads. It is ideally suited for large-scale databases, high-throughput static web serving, and data-intensive analytical processing.
description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.12xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 48 dedicated ARM64 physical cores powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors running at 2.8 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 384 GB of RAM and a massive 30,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, alongside 75 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. The instance delivers top-tier performance in multi-core benchmarks, memory bandwidth, and database operations, though single-core execution and small-model LLM text generation represent performance tradeoffs. This hardware profile offers high qualitative cost efficiency for resource-dense, storage-heavy deployments. It is highly suited for large-scale databases, multi-threaded data processing, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.12xlarge is a storage-optimized ARM64 instance featuring 48 dedicated Graviton cores, 384 GB RAM, and 30,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for high-throughput database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.12xlarge is an ARM64 storage-optimized instance with 48 dedicated cores, 384 GB RAM, and 30,000 GB of local NVMe SSD.
tagline: A high-capacity storage and memory-optimized ARM64 platform featuring massive local NVMe SSD capacity and exceptional multi-threaded database performance.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARM64 cores running at 2.8 GHz
30,000 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
384 GB of system memory with top-tier read and write bandwidth
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the Nitro hypervisor
Excellent fit for large-scale databases and multi-threaded data processing
Tradeoffs include moderate single-core performance and slower small-model LLM text generation
